text: This is great for soldering wires in small electronics, and as a DIYer I'm always a fan of having more solder around. This stuff melts easily and seems to work as described. HOWEVER, I can't vouch for what's in it. The Amazon listing says Lead free, but I've gotten products that were mislabeled in their listing before. The fact that this comes on a spool with no markings or indications makes me a little wary, but I've worked with lead solder before as well, so I just treat it with the care and concern I would if I did know it was lead. Be careful with it. As far as solder goes, it works!
Works as intended.
It does NOT glow like the picture shows at all.  It is a brown volleyball trophy like thing.  So disappointed.
Now I need to paint my walls.

Kinda wobbles on carpet. It looks tall in the photo, but it isn't all that tall. The top panels are a bit thick so the light is very dim. It can't support a 100 watt bulb, so the light would be more for a dark corner or at the end of a hall, not for reading or brightening up a room.

This product is really fun! I used it to grab something that fell inside a hard-to-reach area, which was really useful.  Would be pretty cool for jewelry that falls into a drain pipe or a screw that drops inside something. And that's what it really is for. But it's also a fun and quirky thing to carry around with you! I used mine at a dinner table with family when I asked for the salt (I said "oh wait, I'll get it myself" and pulled this out of my pocket and extended and picked up the metal salt shaker- there was equal amounts laughter and eye rolls all around!). My kids loved playing with it too, trying to steal each other's forks and spoons from the table... until unfortunately the telescoping part snapped. To be fair, my 4 year old was using it when that happened, and this realistically isn't a kid's toy so I can't fault the product for that. When I used it as intended, it did work fine. But I wish it was a little bit more durable, so 4 stars.
This tenderizer works well if your meat isn't very thick.  If it is thick, it doesn't do as well of a job.  We use it for thin filets.

These gloves are horrible. I’ve been buying plainsman gloves for years at Sams Club and been totally satisfied. Decided to buy from amazon and what a difference. No two gloves in a pair were the same. The index fingers are all too long and that makes it almost impossible to work in them. They were even sized differently. Total disappointment. Maybe India isn’t a great place to manufacture them.
Extremely bright
Works so well it broke the cheap, Chinese, pipe under my new sink!

I wanted to use this to repurpose some old furniture that has been through a lot- a buffet table that has had the finish ruined by candle wax, cup stains and food spills, etc. And another little shelf by the front door that keys often get thrown on which has caused scuffs and scrapes in the finish. I've seen amazing things done with some paint and contact paper, and wood colored wallpaper like this seems like a good option. But here's the thing- in person, it does NOT look like wood at all. It doesn't look classy or professional, it actually looks more like the kind of printed pattern you'd expect on wrapping paper. My wife took one look at it and nixed my project, saying it looked better as is than this cartoony print.

On the flipside, the adhesive seems good and the waterproof claim can come in handy in the right application. But the product photos implied this is good for furniture refurbishing and honestly, I don't think I can recommend it for that. 3 stars at best.

For the price they charge, I thought they'd better made.  They are very flimsy and several of them already had punctured holes.  I should have bought them from Walmart for $1 each.  The metal part holding the lantern is way too long. Very disappointed.
Lousy lousy sink stopper. Doesn't work at all

The tiles are easy to adjust size, but it doesn't look great after application. From the picture, it looked more like tile--it isn't. It's PVC and it looks like PVC. this stuff is really NOT worth the money you pay for it.

This rainfall shower head is very nice. It was pretty easy to install and the instructions were easy to follow. The water pressure is not the best in the world, but I chose to keep the water restrictor on to help save water. I haven’t tried it yet without it.

The rainfall feature feels nice, but I did have to use the sprayer to rinse out the shampoo and conditioner as the shower head alone did not have enough pressure. It takes some getting used to when you have had a regular shower head all of these years.

The price is high, but it’s very elegant and will save money on your water bill in the long run. I like it.
I bought this based on the positive reviews online but this didn’t work for my sliding door. The screws for the base are too long and block my sliding door from moving. Consider alternative designs

I have an entertainment system I've been designing in my minivan that works using an Android head unit and game console with a switch routing between 2 screens (each can display one or share sources). Without going into too much detail here, it's a complex system and I haven't worked out all the bugs. I occasionally need to just reboot everything in the back when something goes wrong or gets frozen, which tends to happen on long road trips.
I was looking for something like this, a way to remotely turn off the system if something needs to be rebooted, but all the ones I found previously were designed for 120v AC outlets. I wanted just a DC remote shut off!
This product is perfect because it can be connected to the car's 12v wiring which supplies power to the rear equipment, and the remote switch can be mounted near the driver, and a secondary one in the back for the kids to do themselves if they need to and don't want to bother me while driving. So far it seems to work, I'm pretty happy with it. The only improvement I'd suggest is that if it doesn't have wire terminals built in, at least it should come with some quick connect splice boxes or something. I don't want to permanently solder it while testing it out, and wire nuts are cumbersome and unnecessary for such limited current. But as far as functionality goes, it does what it says so I'd give it 5 stars.
Doesnt work

So I'm giving this 2 stars. For the most part it did cover evenly, but there is some slight patches of discoloration. I dont like it but I can live with it. I would say of my approx 180 sq ft of patio about 25% is a discolored patch. It sprayed well they my sprayer and dried to touch quickly. My Huge complaint is it did not even give me 1 coat over my 180sq ft. I ran out with about a 4 x 4 ft section. No where near the 1500 sq ft it claims. I use a standard pump sprayer, it didnt coat thick or spray enough to puddle so I'm not sure what happened there. I didnt want to wait for a week or so for another bottle so I bought another gallon locally of another brand and of course you can tell.

My recommendation is if you get this, order at least 2 gallons more than you think you will need. And be prepared to live with what you get.

I was so excited about this Worx portable mini blower, as I have a Worx leaf blower and it works amazing. However, upon opening, I realized that it was only the tool itself included. I could have sworn when I ordered it, that it also included a power supply, however now it appears that the verbiage has changed to “tool only” upon receiving the item today.

I quickly looked online to find the power supply, however I realized that it requires a charger, a hub, and a battery. All of these separate items cost approximately (depending on battery power) from $120 to $160 extra on top of the mini blower cost itself. So if you wanted this particular 1 item, it would cost close to $200. My full size leaf blower by Worx cost much less ($59.99 retail) than this and is a lot more powerful.

Unfortunately I am not willing to spend an extra $120-$160 to get this little item to work, so I honestly cannot test it out.

Another thing to add- it’s truly not even portable, as you would still have to make sure the power supply is attached with the battery, so you will be constantly hauling around a “tail” with your blower. On the other hand, where the blower component is, it is completely separate from the handle and to where the power supply goes to - There is enough room for a 3.7 volt battery with a USB charge which would be sufficient for this size of an item. If this were done, it would be 100% portable, convenient, and more cost efficient. They would just have to add a speed control to the blower itself if this were done.

You can purchase a bundle with all of the tools and power supplies for cheaper. But if you purchase these a la carte, then it becomes very costly for just this 1 item.

I will just have to use my full sized leaf blower to get the small areas I was intending to use this on.

They aren't bad when they work. They did come with batteries but they die very quickly. I'd suggest having a fresh set so you can use them when you need them. Commit to where you stick them because the adhesive is surprisingly strong. I can see someone breaking the closure trying to un-stick it.
These are very scratchy and irritating on the face.

It took a while to get someone to install it for me, but oh how wonderful it is to have. Smooth and quiet. the light covers are okay, nothing fancy, but that's okay, the breeze is worth it. I recommend this to anyone, but you do need to have it professionally installed.

Nice bag! That's about the only positive thing I'd say - all else is neutral or negative. I bought two different models to see which one fits my needs better. Firstly, this one's fairly big comparetively - well bigger than iPhone 6 and the competition. Also, loud! even at the lowest setting. The battery, after a full charge, ran for 3.5 hours nonstop at lowest setting, and it woul have continued, had I not turned it off after wife complained how loud it is. 5 speed settings, at the fastest it's high pitched and annoying. Has a useless light which you can turn on and off with button, but the light doesn't cover work area as it's blocked by the chuck. You can hear components banging around when you shake it up and down and side to side. Also, it gets hot right under the name label, because nobody bothered to engineer vent holes. The mini USB (yeah, 2000 called wants the interface back) cable is nice and decently long, white, so you can see it better in low light. The HARDELL unit has skinny haptic buttons, and the other has a tactile one - actual plastic piece - I noticed on some other reviews that the skinny button "tape" starts tp peel off as well as wears out over time - especially in high dust and grit environments - meaning, it's not engineered to last.

Did I mention it comes in a nice zip bag? Yeah, but that's not my bag baby... Populo is quieter, smaller, doesn't get as hot, doesn't rattle, costs the same, and even though it doesn't have a light and is only three speeds, for me at least, came on top. So orange palooka's going back in it's body bag (hopefully back to the drawing board as well, cause it does have potential)

Nothing dramatic, very low tech and minimal features but it does work.  And easy enough to program that my cat sitter and emergency contact can each have one.  CAUTION the button is sensitive so if you plan to carry it around in a purse or backpack or keep a spare in a drawer MAKE SURE it cannot get bumped or you may accidentally open the door.
They don’t work. I tried charging it with the usb and nothing. I do not recommend this product of company. The company has horrible customer service

Not quite what I expected and hoped for.  Yes, it's a nightlight.  BUT, it is very dim, and my sole purpose in choosing this nightlight was the ability to plug something else in to that light plug without removing the nightlight.  I could find no prongs that fit that plug, so I still have to remove the nightlight to use a curling iron or blowdryer.  Don't know what prongs fit that - I couldn't get anything to work.
Fits nicely over bulb.  Much better than showing bare bulb. Defuses light nicely. One shade came cracked.  The package it came in was good.

I can't vouch for the accuracy of the color here, but usually 5000-5500k is considered sunlight color. When I work with cameras and want to match the look of window light, that's usually the range I work in (compared to tungsten/yellow light, which is approximately 2700 kelvin). The bulb is plenty bright at 1200 lumens, but unless you're using it combined with natural sunlight I don't really like the color. It is way too cool of a color, especially if anything in your house is closer to the more traditional yellow / warm colored lights. See the attached picture of it in the same lamp as a 3000k LED bulb. It is very blue next to it, and feels jarringly intense as a result, despite actually putting out LESS light (the other bulb is a 1500 lumen LED).
All in all, I think I'd prefer 3000k colored lights that put out more light versus this very blue light at less lumens. But some people might like this, especially if they've already decided they like "full spectrum" daylight colored light and have changed all the bulbs in their house to this color range. It definitely seems to be what it says on the box, 6500k and 1200 lumens, so if you know what you are getting and know that's what you want, I say go for it! If you aren't sure, go with 3000k colored lights instead.

I just ran out of my LED bulbs and bought these. They work great and are very bright. Price is about the same as anyone else selling them. We use nothing but LED's in our home and it knocked our electric bill way down. Highly recommend everyone changes over to them.
Functional lock but very inconvenient tiny dial numbers
The wallpaper is beautiful, but it rips and tears really easy and it’s not very sticky. After two days, this was already falling off of my wall. Don’t buy this, you’ll be sorry.

I’m not sure what’s going on here.  It’s advertised as LED but appears to act more like a fluorescent bulb.  Time will tell.  So far, it fits under my microwave over the range and gives off a bright light.  I haven’t checked to see whether it remains cooler than the incandescent light bulb next to it.  As I said, time will tell whether it’s efficient and long-lasting.  The incandescent bulbs provide a low bar.

I didn't like it at first. It is long and skinny and how could this little thing put out enough light?

I was wrong. I really do like it. it never gets in the way, puts out just the right amount of light for what ever I am doing, and even has a nighlight mode which I love--no more tripping over sprawling cats. The little knob at the end switches to brighter light at the touch of a finger--then switches between kinds of light. No question, this thing may look really modern and might not fit your decor, but it is a worthy lamp for office, work station, hand work, computer work, or watching TV. Love it!
Worked as expected.

Enclosed paperwork lists an email address for product registration, but the email address is UNSAFE according to all my browsers, basically they say DO NOT PROCEED. So this seller has sold me an expensive tool and I cannot register the product with the manufacturer. When I contacted them, they told me they can't send me any web addresses per Amazon policy. So caveat emptor, buying from this seller means you cannot register your Disto. So 1/2 star for the seller.

The actual Disto so far is lovely. I've used it to measure, and this week I will be using Bluetooth to connect with Total for Mobile so I can send the measurements directly to the sketch!

This is a nice looking rainfall shower head. It looks produces a wide, even spray. However according to the hardness testing strips I bought here on amazon, the water coming out of it is basically identical to what comes out without a shower head attached. See if you can tell which strip is which ... I can't .
Takes for ever to get adjusted and still has play.. not worth the price very disappointed

I love these for working around the house. I was carrying wood for a shed project outside and doing light gardening and found them much more comfortable to wear vs classic work gloves, plus they have much more dexterity than I was expecting (great for things like picking up small screws etc).
Also excellent that you three in the package, easily 5 stars!
Bought this for a small Harley Davidson lamp that our daughter gave her dad. The shade is narrow and regular LED bulbs stuck out the top. Found this through a search and bingo!!! Works great!

I had some tungsten G4 bulbs in a chandelier and decided to change them out for these. I have to be honest, these are a little more yellow than the ones I replaced, I feel like I'd rather they have been 3000k instead of 2700k, but the energy savings is significant. These run much cooler and use much less energy, I'm happy with it for the money so far!
Really adds the finishing touches to my daughter's chandelier! Looks beautiful and is the perfect size!

Every is right about these masks but the size!!! Pictured here is my 9yo son and 6yo daughter wearing them.
It's to bad too, because we all really liked these. They fabric is high quality and they're sown together well. They even passed the "candle blow test". But these masks are just too big for children. They fit my husband and I, so they will technically still useful.... But I feel silly wearing them because of the emoji print.

Way not worth the money or your time. For this to cover a flat drain in my shower to collect long hair that me and my daughter continously shed.  The thing floats in anything over a 1/4th in water and after a week it hasnt collected a single hair and I think it allowes the hair to just flow under it. I hate to waste even my time to take it to the ups store but this place isn't getting 10 bucks from me for a useless product.

When doing wood work or lawn care, I usually wear a pair of generic Home Depot style work gloves. They are thick and heavy and don't have a lot of versatility, but they get the job done. I tried these, and now I'm upset at all the time I never tried something better!
These are SO MUCH more comfortable, they feel lighter and you have the dexterity to hold something without fear or splinters or sharp thorns/nails digging into your skin.

I'm very pleased with these and would highly recommend them!
I didn’t get to use them for my couch. The screws were too wide.

We bought this to use in our book safe we also bought at the same time. We can only assume that it works. We put it in our BlueDot Trading Dictionary that has a fireproof metal safe inside. Together we hope it will keep some valuables safe in case of firs (God forbid).
Makes cutting wire a little easier rather than using my dikes or linemans. I liked the price

I bought this to use in our home office.  Eh....it's okay.  I didn't like that it gets hot really fast.  I went to move it & ouch.....not good!  It's not worth returning since I can use it.  But overall, not bad.

I replaced an older light fixture in my daughter's room with this, and it's pretty great! She can set timers, colors, brightness, all from the remote! The installation was a little bit difficult. It includes next to no helpful directions, and sliding the panel over the plate was harder than expected because you can't see that well behind it. I had to try more than once before I felt like it was on correctly.
The construction is very plastic, so it could be better- but once installed, it honestly looked and worked fine. The remote implies that there is a wifi setup but that does not seem to exist. You basically need the remote, there are no manual controls to change any of the settings if you lose it.

I'm giving these solar lights a 3 stars because it's modern and beautiful but the only issue I have with it, is that it does not last all night. And it's not because it not getting full Sunlight. I live in the Carribbean were it is it gets sunlight from 6 am to 6 pm.
This handy dandy chain saw is light and easy to hold above the head.Easy to grip so no slipping. A great addition to my garden tool collection. It makes easy work of fallen limbs after storms.

[[VIDEOID:8e62a242e0017c11fa1c0532c56a5f16]] I love all the options for sockets this comes with- Screwdriver or wrench style handles, and various bits including deep socket ones. What I don't like, however, is the case. I can't do it justice in writing, so I'm including a video review to explain better.
Bottom line: Good tools, but be careful with the case. Or better yet, if you have a tool shelf or tool bag to use instead of this case, maybe you'd prefer that.
They are ok. I expected better
Bought this for my hubby. We are building a tiny home and this tool is a huge help

The plugs seem fine when you look at them.  on my recent trip to Rome, they did not work as expected.  the outlets are smaller in diameter than what It thought.  these plugs are thicker in diameter and would not enter the socket properly.  I was prepared with older styles plugs since this was the 1ST time I used these.  I now know that when in Italy, leave them behind!

There is another light fixture from this brand that has two bulbs in a larger base, but I was a bit disappointed to see that the 11" one only supports a single bulb. Especially when used with an LED such as it is here (which is more directional in light emitting than a classic incandescent). it seems very lopsided in glow.
But I used a couple of these to replace an ugly older fixture that came with the house and some "fixtures" after construction in our hallway that were basically a single bulb in the ceiling. At least this looks nicer than that, but mostly when off. I'm kind of disappointed in how the single bulb looks in a round fixture like this.
Description says they have an adjustable nose clip, but they don’t.

Great surge protector! The 3-foot cord is just the right length. It is easy to use, easy to turn off and on with your finger or your toe :) I recommend this to anyone who needs a good surge protector.

We just finished building a larger tiny house and put these bulbs in the two outside lights on either side of the door to keep bugs from congregating around the door. They work very well and they are very pretty to boot!!! Give a lovely yellow glow around the area at night.

This is an excellent way to add lights without running wires. I like that this is rechargeable so you don't need to replace batteries like other closet/counter lighting, and the motion sensor means you can have it only turn on when needed and stay off when you don't to prolong time between charges. The magnetic base holds on pretty well, and makes it easy to mount/remount, however the caveat to me is the buttons. There are two buttons, one marked like a power button and the other seems to have an icon of a light/sun. It makes it hard to understand which button does what without referring to the instructions. I have a similar model from another brand that uses a switch instead of the dual buttons here, and that switch seems much more intuitive to understand and use. That's my only caveat, otherwise a great product!
Keeps breaking and leak cold air in the house, don't buy it.
Too wide for my gutter.

I appreciate this innovative, curly, artsy thing with light. It is intensely bright to night-light-dim adjustable. Great! But it can be annoyingly in your eyes bright. This is the kind of light you'd put on a sideboard, living room end table, or by the back door where you want light for someone to walk through the room without bumping into something. Or set it on a guest bathroom cabinet to let folks know where the lou is. I can't read by it, and while watching TV it's okay, but a lamp that is higher up doesn't get in the eyes like this one does that sits at eye level. It definitely lights up a dark corner.

It is white. That makes it perfect to go with my white kitchen decor.

AND.. it's pretty. It's unique. It could start a conversation. And it is useful to put by the back door so when I come home the house is lit rather than dark. I like that a lot.

This filter has so much potential! Pros:

+ Water passes through it faster than any other filter I've used before
+ It looks cool!
+ It comes with a sweet carrying case

However there are two major cons that I think outweigh the good:

- THERE'S NO VENT! The thing is designed as though you should put it on top of the bottle you're filling and just leave it there, but since there is no channel for air to pass through, the flow slows to a trickle unless you hold it above the container to allow air to get in. A simple notch cut into the spout would solve this. I'm going to attempt to make my own.
- It's pretty awkward to hold, which is a problem because I always have to hold it to actually use it

I just replaced a whole box of socket wrench parts with this ONE! I love the way this works- place it over a bolt, for example, and it presses in on the rods inside to form a shape around the head, thus being a custom size fit on the fly. No longer needing to trial-and-error fit the right mm size to the bolt I'm trying to work with!
It works pretty well, however there's one caveat I've noticed: If you have multiple parts too close to each other, it might be harder to use than an exact size socket bit. I was trying to tighten a base plate for a child bicycle carrier, and I found it difficult to turn individual bolt heads next to one another since the head was too wide. It bumped into the ones next to it and make it hard to turn individual ones. But otherwise, for everything else I've truly appreciated having this "master wrench" one size for all!

I really like this lamp. It arrived as expected. Unfortunately, the bulb blew in a matter of hours, making it a more expensive and hassle-filled purchase than it should have been.  No reason to buy online if I end up having to go to the store to replaced the bulb. Some items that require specialty-sized bulbs include a spare in the package...this would have been a great idea.
Light is great but double side tape/magnets didn’t hold and the light fell and broke. Slightly disappointed
What luxury to have a lamp that has different light levels. It has a neat clamp you can attach the lamp to the desk so it doesn't move around and you can't knock it off the desk. Love it.

I got these intending to use them for all sorts of things, but when they arrived they are SO TINY they won't go around anything but maybe a small cable. So beware, they are not as big as they look and will only help if you want to secure very small bundles of things.

I wanted this to turn on and off a device I have installed in my crawl space, and it works perfectly for that purpose! The remote easily connects and disconnects the plug through walls and a decent amount of distance. I love that it also comes with a keychain remote, it's handy when I misplace the regular one, and I could see this being very useful on things like garage lights and the like.
The one caveat I have, is that the shape of the receiver plug is a bit too tall to allow anything to plug in next to it. In other words, this is fine as long as you don't have anything else plugged into the outlet you intend to use it with. Otherwise, you can get around this limitation with an extension cord or power strip adapter, etc. I'm happy with it for my purposes and recommend it.

The holder doesn't hold properly. This is not the cheap one on the market, so we expected better quality than what we received. We need to buy another holder to hold it properly. Very bad design. -- The two flow settings is great. Still thinking if we're returning it.

I used to borrow a neighbor's Little Giant brand ladder for things around and outside my home. I love the collapsible A-frame style because you can make it any height you need in between the min and max, and even use it on stairs to create a level climbing area as well. This is a very interesting and different take on that.
Rather than the large metal hooks which lock the extension in place, each rung here has dual plastic levers that lock in place as you expand. The advantage here is that it is REALLY easy to expand the ladder- just lift as the locks engage rung by rung. Stop when it is the correct height. The problem, however, is that you can't lock a leg in a shorter position- as soon as you lift it off the ground, gravity will attempt to pull it out further. In fact, when collapsing the ladder, you need to use the built in strap to hold it closed and prevent it from trying to open as you carry it away. I wish there was a way to lock the legs in place like the Little Giant and its variants.
Otherwise, it is very useful to have around!

I'm trying out a Nest, And the first thing that we noticed is that the old thermostat had a wider mounting plate, so the holes were still visible in the wall with the Nest installed. Also, since our walls are white The Nest looked a little bit out of place. I did not want to spackle and paint over the holes in case we decide to go back to the old one when the seasons change.
This cover helped alleviate both problems. It conveniently covered the holes, and gives a more decoratory boost around the Nest so that it blends better into the walls.
The only downside however, is that the pattern on it reminds me very much of a paper disposable plate. Interestingly, the material seems to be a soft TPU which you can squish with your fingers. This is probably helpful to seal out any air in the cavity behind the wall for insulation.

All in all I'm happy with it, however I wish it did not have the intricate pattern.

My 4 year old son loves the hat, but he hates it when I attach the screen. The visor of the hat makes it so the screen cannot come down all the way. I was hoping to use it as an extra precaution during this epidemic, so I was disappointed that the screen doesn't really work. Also it took a long time to ship.
Perfect for timing the flooring I'm putting in. Plenty of power and maneuverability. Glad I chose this one.
This is a nice outdoor light.  It is a great value for the money and so far it works beautifully!

I bought two of these to cover my large fenced compost bin last year and it does a wonderful job. It's been out there now for a year and still looks brand new. Blends in well with my wooded acreage. I just attached it with some of my stainless steel clothespin clips and had it up in a couple of minutes. I'm going to buy two more for another bin I am building.

This takes some getting used to.  In order to get the pressure you are looking for it actually has to be set 1.5-2psi higher than you want.  Once we figured that out it was ok.  Better than waiting at the gas station for air in our tires.

This item was my mother-in-law’s requested present from my husband last Christmas as she recently had a hand surgery. She said this was the best gift she ever received. It helps her a lot in doing the dishes.

Only good for small jobs. If you want a sprayer to paint anything large like a home look elsewhere. This is from our professional painter who was painting our home recently. It does not give out a goodly amount of paint as needed for big jobs.

Phillips makes some decent brand name household equipment, and these bulbs are no different. I like the way you can adjust them but it doesn't require wifi or an app or anything like that... It's a physical dial that you set and forget, and it has plenty of in between settings so you can literally dial in the color you need between tungsten and daylight. Well made, would recommend.
I'm old and forgetful. These remind me quickly to turn off my kitchen sink faucet if I walk away. Very loud!!!!!! Highly recommend.😁👍🏻

I put this in my motion detector spotlight​ in my back yard. It isn't as bright as the old one, but it does light up the place enough to make intruders run for cover. It is not hot to touch after it has been on a while. I think this is a good product. I'm glad I ordered it.
The mask is too tight. I am looking for a suitable recipient to gift them to.

This is good securing low grade items, such as a food pantry that I volunteer in. Using this chain and set of locks we are able to secure some cabinets and refrigerators that were being raided before food distributions. I've seen more industrial locks on the market, but for light duty use like lockers, panties, bikes, etc, this is enough to slow someone down enough to not be worth breaking in.
For the asking price, I think it is worth the money.

Personally I wasn't that impressed by the quality of this tiara. The necklace a and bracelet is okay quality, but the tiara simply looks cheap. It looks like it is made from glitter foam. For the $12 price tag this was listed at the time I wrote the review, I would have been disappointed with this purchase. I don't think I can recommend this set.

You can calibrate this thing all day, and you will if you're going to try and get an accurate reading. It's cheap, but you'll waste more money in calibration solution trying to get it right then just spending more up front on a better meter. The only reason I can think this has such high rating is people aren't rechecking them.

After continued use my opinion has changed, I leave it in a shotglass with the calibration solution and it seems it doesn't require recalibration as often. Maybe once or twice a week it will begin to read .1 too high and will need to be adjusted.

7/5/2014 You get what you pay for with this product, it costs about as much as a couple bottles of calibration solution. And you'll be using alot of solution, about 6 months ago I bought a BlueLabs Combo meter and I never looked back, yeah it's quite pricey but you get what you pay for, every 30 days it needs to be readjusted and it has a icon on the display to remind you, no dial, automatic calibration. The probe needs to be kept wet, their say is "if it dries it dies" and it has a cleaning kit to use on it every month, maybe that's the issue with the Etekcity, maybe if it was cleaned and stored in a KCL solution it would work better longer. I don't know but I love my BlueLabs Combo so far.

These were bought as a replacement for the burnt original incandescent bulb. Now, with a benefit of a hindsight, it is clear that LEDs (or maybe most LEDs) cannot be used inside a microwave. Alas, this seller never indicated that and I started an additional research after they were, one by one, destroyed by the magnetron within a few seconds of starting the microwave. Actually, it looks like most sellers conveniently forgot to mention that fact. But, a few do have a warning that they can only be used outside of a microwave (like in those over-the-oven ones). Likely, as long as these are not used in a microwave, they should be fine. Alas, I cannot comment on the longevity of that usage.  Total lifetime of all 3 in my case was about 5 seconds :-)

I had a Moen system previously in this shower that was acting up. For a long time now I wasn't able to close the water, even after changing the cartridge, handle, and most of the system. Eventually it wouldn't turn on anymore, so I gave up and went the nuclear approach to try a total replacement off Amazon.
I had this installed a couple of days ago, and so far I really like it. Everything works smoothly and effortlessly, on and off, hot and cold, exactly as it should. And best of all, when I turn it off, it STAYS OFF, no dripping. Perfect.
I can't speak for longevity, that's the part that I'm nervous about but so far so good. It actually has some things the name brand Moen components did not, such as a rubber gasket around both the inner ring as well as the outer one (the Moen only had the outer rubber ring). That means hopefully it is less likely to get water into the mechanism or wall behind the tiles. I was surprised the name brand one did not have this.
I'll try to come back and update this review if I ever have issues with it.

I got this because I wanted a more "rainfall" shower head, while still having the handheld option. In that, this  fits the bill.  It took longer than any other showerhead to install (in my experience) due to the weird slip nut that is the first thing instructed to install.  This piece ended up preventing me from being able to fully attach the actual diverter head to the arm. Once I removed it, I was able to install the diverter piece with no leakage. My shower arm is higher than standard, and it's not very long, so this head sits almost on the wall--so if you have a short arm, might consider an extender.  My handheld would not sit in the arm due to this lack of space, but that's fine with me as the previous owners installed a holder half way down the wall, and that's where mine will sit.  I have a little trouble switching the diverter as it's stiff (and very high, but the second part is not the fault of the manufacturer). I did remove the flow limiter, and I'm happy with the amount of water that comes through the larger head.  There's not quite as great a result when you use both, because you are splitting water, but I usually use the handheld for spot rinsing or cleaning the tub, so it won't need a lot of both going at the same time.  Overall, not a bad showerhead for the price, just wish it hadn't been so difficult to install and that it gave a little more room from the shower arm.

I have to say, I'm impressed. My old craftsman drill set could use a new battery, but this whole kit costs less than a single replacement battery! Chances are I don't have to say this if you're looking at an e39 mount bulb, but just in case it needs to be said: This is NOT for use in a standard light socket (usually referred to as an e26 Edison type). Yes, they sell adapters, and I've tried them for use with my video softbox to get more light output in a portable package. But I don't recommend them. I was actually hoping to do something like that here, but generally, E39 bulbs are larger and heavier, and assume you have the thicker mount to hold it securely. The adapters rely on the smaller diameter socket to hold the bigger one which not only could have ruined my light box as it started to bend, but could have fallen out and made a real mess. That being said, this bulb IS super bright and puts out the light equivalent of equipment that usually requires several times the amount of energy. The most common point of failure in LED bulbs, however, is heat dissipation since unlike a traditional tungsten, halogen or vapor bulb, there are integrated circuits here which can easily burn out at high temperatures. The bulb therefore has an active cooling system, with vents and a whirring fan that you can hear if you are too close to it (yet another reason it would not work as a video lamp, although perhaps for continuous light photography).
That concerns me regarding longevity because it means there are moving parts which can eventually wear out and stop spinning, unlike traditional passive cooling LED bulbs. Then again, a bulb with this many diodes would not be possible without active cooling, so that's the compromise I suppose.

For what it is, it is a good balance of supreme light output for commercial/industrial level use, while saving a ton on energy compared the 1,000watt bulb it claims to be equivalent to.

This is a great smart thermostat that integrates with GE's Sync app and smart ecosystem. I'm kind of shocked at the other reviews claiming they were surprised it wasn't compatible with their older wiring. Much like almost any other modern Wifi-enabled thermostat, you need a common wire (C-wire) for a constant source of power in order to keep this on and connected. In other words, if you can't install a Nest or other smart thermostat, and instead have been stuck using with AA batteries that you need to change... this is going to need a wiring update! All of them do!
Thankfully, this is already standard and any house made in recent years will have it. Also, there are plenty of kits on the market to "add a C-wire" for those who need it. When the Nest came out, my old house did not support it, however when we moved, one of the things I wanted to make sure of was that our home had more updated HVAC wiring so that I could install a smart thermostat. If you've got the wiring, it is easy as anything to install! Just follow the diagram and keep track of your color-coded wires, and everything should work exactly as expected! I've seen GE's Sync system for light bulbs and smart plugs/switches, and they tend to be rated quite well so this is an interesting addition to their lineup!

This is a great clamp on light for bedside reading use. The other thing is I wish the switch were located on the line instead of on the cable. If I mount this to a desk or bed side, I want to hide the wire behind the furniture. As it is now, I have to fish the wire back out to hit the switch. Its a minor inconvenience, the light is otherwise wonderful and I'm enjoying it. We like to have parties outside on the deck for friends and family, and lights like these are awesome to have around. I've tried a few different similar panels, and I love that you can dial in specific colors to match a theme, or let it cycle on its own for a crazy dance vibe. There's no program control beyond the remote so you can't really sync it up with other lights or anything, but that's to be expected at this price point.
The one thing that disappointed me, however, was the brightness. Not that they aren't bright- they get PLENTY bright! But I have 100w LED flood lights that I've used before, and they get brighter than this. I think that because this is a color changing setup, the full power is split between varying RGB levels and you therefore never get the full power of a solid color 100w LED panel. If you get a single color LED at 100 watts, it will be noticeably brighter than this (like, fill my backyard with stadium light levels of bright). This is plenty of light to set the mood, but keep your lumens expectations realistic.

These aren't working for me. I ordered them to hang around a full length mirror that is on the back of a bedroom door, in an area that has poor lighting. But as far as lighting goes some how they're not very illuminating.... yet they're incredibly hard to look directly at. A lot like LED xmas lights.
What I mean by that is the light they give off doesn't reach very far. The light extends out maybe six inches, at best, before it starts evaporating. So while it lights up the area directly around the mirror, me standing in the outfit I want to look at not even two feet away I am barley lit up. Yet because they're LED lights, they're hard to look at. Being on the mirror itself, it's impossible not to squint when looking in their direction.

I've used a few similar looking smart plugs in my home, but this is the best one I've gotten by far. You just can't beat an actual Amazon branded product for their ecosystem!
For starters, each outlet is individually controlled. I had one previously that was all-or-nothing, but this will allow you to either group or control each plugged in device individually. Nice.
Secondly, no third party app as a layer in between!
Every other smart plug or switch I've used required an app to be installed on my phone, then you can choose to have Alexa set up with the specific skill for it. Sometimes that app is kind of sketchy, and appears to be poorly translated and/or required to get from an untrusted source. With this, no third party is required- it connects and works directly within the Alexa app already and does not require anything else. It's like getting the name brand accessory for a product! It just works, and well at that!
Some people have pointed out that it doesn't work with other systems (Google home, Siri, etc), but that's sort of obvious, I'm not sure why someone would think an Amazon product designed for Alexa would be compatible with the other programs.
Highly recommended!

I have some PEX pipes that the previous owner of my home did some weird things to, so I wanted to cut out and redo some of it. This kit was great help, it has elbows and corners and straight connection parts, so I was able to mix-and-match to get all the crimped connections I needed. I had to borrow a crimping tool for it, but it was pretty easy to figure out and use for my first attempt. Everything is holding solid and strong, and most importantly, leak free!
The only downside in my opinion, is the strange number of items in the package. You get 10 of each type of fitting, which means 30 connection fittings, but also only 10 rings to crimp it down. A single connection requires two rings (one on each side), and one of those T fittings requires 3. You will end up only being able to make a maximum of 5 connections with this, and as little as 3 if you're using all the triple T connections. Really, it should come with more rings, otherwise you have a bunch of brass fittings left over that you can't do anything with!

This is a pretty cool looking lamp, and it does a good job lighting up my son's work area on his desk. It gets plenty bright, and has three different color modes to match whatever ambient light you want (sunlight, indoor tungsten colored lights, and a blend of the two). It has a little fan built in that can blow a small draft of air towards you if you like, but don't expect this to circulate the air in a room or anything like that. It is more like a personal breeze in case your desk area feels stuffy. I think it is kind of silly but my son actually likes it so, points for that I guess! The timer function means you can also use it as a sort of night light, although I think if it had more options to set it would be more useful. Right now, it just has a timer on or off setting. You can't even adjust how long you want it to be.
I also have mixed opinions about the light up control dial. It lights up even when everything is off, which is useful to find it in the dark, however this is part of why I feel it is better suited in a kids room. I wouldn't want that light always glowing at night in an adult / college dorm room, personally. For kids, it's cool and almost like a glow in dark / nightlight sort of thing.
Otherwise, as a desk lamp, I think it works well and it has a really modern and cool design.

This is an impressive amount of light for a bulb small enough to fit in a standard E26 base. I have used large LEDs inside of a softbox for photography but usually for this much output you need a larger bulb that requires a wider base for support (E39). This actually comes with adapters for that in case you want to use a a fixture made for a larger base, but the beauty here is that this will natively fit in a standard light socket made for traditional sized (E26) bulbs.
Since it comes in a set of 2, I was able to put them into matching softbox kits to light a subject with light that is similar in color to window light.
I say similar because I actually found this has a bit of the green spike that some LEDs have compared to photographer bulbs in the 5000+ Kelvin spectrum. If you want to avoid that, you might need to get more expensive bulbs made for photo/video use. But for high output bulbs that fit in a standard socket, this is a great value for the price! In my experiments with it so far, it was close enough to mix with window light in photography.

This is pretty near PERFECT solution to a real problem we had! We recently bought a home that had motion sensor lights for the front walkway which frequently misfired and turned themselves on when not wanted (wind blowing a tree branch, neighbor's dog, etc). There was no switch associated with these lights, so we looked to add one.
This product is MUCH cheaper than hiring an electrician and MUCH easier than opening up the walls to run a wire to a switch! It comes with little crimp cable connections that easily attach to the existing wiring, much simpler than wire nuts and fast too... which I'm thankful for since I need to do this while balancing on a ladder! And once connected, each unit can be paired with any of the 3 buttons on the remote control switch included (even more than one- which I'll get to in a minute).
Here's the caveat, however: These are NOT pre-paired. I didn't know that. The first time I tried to wire one up, I closed everything up, turned the power back on, tried all the buttons and nothing worked. I had to actually unmount the light AGAIN to get to this receiver and press the button to put into pairing mode. Make sure you do that before putting everything back in the wall and remounting the fixture. It's annoying only because you need the power on in order to pair, and I usually only turn my breaker back on once the wires are put back (and I can't reach the receiver anymore).
So what I actually did is take an appliance plug I happened to have lying around in my garage, and I wired THAT up to the receivers, and plugged each one into an outlet to pair first. Once paired with a button, I went out and installed the other two (I learned my lesson after the first one).
An interesting feature to note is that each button on the remote switch panel puts out an RF signal, and each receiver in the wall can be paired with more than one. For example, first I paired one with the wrong button, so I did it again with the other button, only to find that now BOTH buttons would toggle power on that light. You have to press-and-hold to clear all previous programming first if you don't want 2 buttons doing the same light. Then again, this opens up some interesting possibilities, such as one button toggling all three lights, where as other buttons can do individual power if needed, and therefore make nested groups of control.
If they came pre-programmed to buttons on the remote (but could still be re-programmed if desired), I'd have given this product 5 stars. Since I had to take it out and pair it after installation, I'm removing a star.

I have a few light fixtures in my home that require these smaller bulbs, and the problem has traditionally been that they don't come in bright enough sizes. Unless you want to get the "corn cob" style LED, which sometimes looks funny without a diffuser.
This is some sort of magic bulb that resembles a traditional tungsten filament inside, but they claim is "100 Watt equivalence" out of only 8 watts. Well, to be fair, a REAL 100 Watt tungsten bulb should output something like 1600 lumens, and this is only rated at 800. There are plenty of other brands that make the same claim so I can't fault them for that, however I have another "100 Watt" LED that puts out 1400-1500 lumens, and you can tell that this is significantly less bright. That being said, I'm impressed at how natural looking these bulbs are for LEDs.
The super bright tiny bulbs usually need to resort to "corncob" style LED formations, which are many little LED points of light that look like... well, corn. And while those work, it doesn't look as nice without a diffusion cover to mask all the bright dots. This type of bulb can work in a fixture with an open bulb design, because they actually look really natural with no cover at all. That's cool. And for the money, you get 6 of them in a neat little package. I like that too. All in all, these seem like a good value and I'd recommend them, as long as you don't actually need 1600 lumens per bulb!

There's a lot I like about this plug. It looks nice and was really easy to set up! I didn't even need to install their app, as it works with the more standard Smartlife app which I already have from other devices in my home. Since Smartlife is already hooked up with my voice assistants (Alexa), I didn't need to do anything else! Once the app found it, it was automatically added as an available plug to control from Alexa. If you don't already have something like this setup, the usual process is to install their app, configure the device, then go to your digital assistant and add the skill for that manufacturer's product (Geeni has its own skill for Alexa). I was able to set up a schedule for it to automatically come on and off at desired times, and it seems to work as expected.
The ONLY issue is, it is on a circuit which gets shut off manually by a wall switch from time to time (the entire room's outlets are on switches). Unlike some other smartplugs I've used, this device will NOT remember what state it was in when it got unplugged. It will default to OFF upon power loss. That isn't so cool. Every time someone shuts the power off to the plug, even if the light was on, when you turn the power back on you'll have to manually go over and press the button on the plug too. Or use Alexa/app/etc. It's an extra unnecessary step that I found annoying.
To be fair, most people don't use it this way and have the plug set somewhere with permanent power going to it. My setup is admittedly atypical, however this is a feature which I've noticed OTHER smart switches can handle so I have to remove at least one star for not working the way I expected. I'll probably swap a different smart plug into that location and use this one somewhere that never gets shut off.

These are great energy efficient bulbs, however I hate the use of "equivalents" as they are so loosely interpreted by vendors in my experience. While I love the color (3000k is the sweet spot) and the amount of energy they use, they are not as bright as other "100 Watt equivalent" products. However, looking at the specs it is easy to see why. These are rated at only 1100 lumens, whereas my brighter LED bulbs produce more like 1500-1600 lumens. That's around 50% more light. Granted, they also consume an extra couple of watts of real power as well, which makes sense. But the numbers are confusing, since 800 lumens is considered a 60-75watt tradition incandescent equivalent. Since no one has ever heard of an 85-90Watt bulb, anything over 1,000 lumens they have to round up to say 100w.
It's all very confusing and honestly, people need to stop using incandescent equivalents and start looking at strictly lumen light-output numbers for comparison. For the stated specifications, I'm happy with this bulb and would recommend it. But don't expect it to be the "brightest" 100w bulb. It's definitely brighter than my "60w" LEDs, which is what I wanted to replace.
I live in the South and bugs abound. Now I can leave my door open and bugs do not fly in or crawl in. It is perfect. It installs really easy. I like it very much!

I tried one of these in my porch to replace an 13w CFL bulb that has been there for years. The old bulb used to attract a lot of bugs, so I was interested in seeing how these "bug repelling" yellow ones fare.
Well, I have to say, there aren't as many bugs around, however I can't say there are none attracted. I did notice a few gnats/mesquitos circling under the light the way they do when they catch a light source. When I turned off the bulb they went elsewhere. So it helps, but isn't completely bug proof.
However I really do like how much more light there is for less power. 650 lumens while only using 8w is an incredible value that will no doubt save me a lot of money down the road. So, I recommend it as a low cost alternative that doesn't repel bugs, but will attract noticeably less.

I've had intermittent backup issues in one of our toilets, so I figured this would be a good place to try a snake auger. I had a really difficult time getting this to work, however. The instructions were not clear and I found it very confusing as a first timer who's only seen professionals use a snake previously. The product doesn't feel all that robust either, but I suppose it doesn't have to be if it works. I wasn't able to get it to move smoothly past the first bend in the toilet and eventually gave up. I did, however, get moderate results removing some hair from a bathtub drain, mostly because it was straight down. It could be that I just don't get how to maneuver into bends of pipes, but I'd think a consumer product could do a better job explaining how to use it properly. Its OK I guess if you know what you are doing. Definitely not newbie friendly.

My previous hose from Delta lasted more than ten (10) YEARS. This one? started gushin like an elephan's trunk in 6 MONTHS. So yeah, go ahead and waste your money on some cheaply made garbage, me? I should have remembered what my grandpa used to say - I'm not rich enough to buy twice. (nor do I have the time to putz around with a new hose installation. Terrible!

Just got them so I can't say anything about the longevity or energy efficiency. However, I do like the brightness. The 60W seems a lot brighter than that in my bathroom light fixture. Nice white light that doesn't wash our my features when trying to put my makeup on.

Piezo lighters are great simple tech with no flammable fuel. But the Ozeri OPL1-R Piezoelectric Stove Lighter is cheaply made and breaks easily or just stops working and has no warrenty. So look for a better brand of Piezo lighter or explore new non-fuel tech that is on the market.

Once installed it looked like it would hold up, sadly after two weeks of use the adhesive came undone. One from the hook side and the other from the wall side. Luckily there was no damage to the wall when it peeled off.
This is crap!  I've been painting furniture for years and I was soooo excited to get this.  Its SUPER STINKY and it makes zero difference over a chalk painted finish
Bought it for my grown son's gaming room at his home. He loved it and it arrived quickly. Great buy!
Online description says they are dimmable. They are not. Online description states they are for indoor and outdoor use. The instructions say they must be fully enclosed. Many porch lights are not.

These are fantastic for low profile ceilings that don't have room for traditional cans. For example, a room with a cathedral style ceiling that lacks an attic space above. I also like that the light emitters are built in and don't require any additional bulb inside to illuminate, however I wonder if that's also a con. LEDs do last a long time, but I have LED Edison bulbs in other light fixtures in the house that have blown from power outages and what not. I wonder how difficult it would be to replace any of these if one blows out, since it is all embedded together. Also, if you decide you want to change the color, right now they sell 3 different k-temperature colors (lower number is more warm color like incandescent,  higher number is more blue like sunlight). There were times that we wanted to try out more sunlight-matching color and switched all our bulbs to 5000k, then went back to a mix with 3000k or 4000k, and I'm a little concerned that with these installed, I'm committed to sticking with this color since it is more difficult to ever change them. You can't select the desired color after the fact, you need to buy the appropriate one. So in some ways, I still prefer real, easily replaced bulbs. But, I do like that this can be installed in places those cannot, and they are surprisingly bright for their size. So 4 stars for being useful enough that the pros outweigh the cons!

great flashlight no doubt, but the every time you turn it off it goes to the next mode setting when you turn it on again.  so basically if you want the light to be at high, and want back in high the next time you have to turn it on and off three more times to cycle through the other modes (medium, strobe, SOS).
I ordered this set of 4 in turquoise and 1 is huge and the other 3 are really small. Really aggravated at the extremely inconsistent size. The color is really muddy also, nothing like the picture.
Making my own furnace filters for my MH HAVC system.

This nun door decoration is super spooky. It is very detailed and looks nice. The only downfall is that it is 2 pieces instead of 1 solid piece. My door is not a flat surface, so it was a bit difficult to get it to line up. Other than it being in 2 separate pieces, it is perfect.
These hold great just don't do what I needed them for.

These bulbs do EXACTLY what the blurb says they'll do. In fact, the light seems a lot brighter than 50 watt eqquivalent. My mom who has macular degeneration (20/60 in one eye and 20/80 in the other) can read her Kindle under just one of these bulbs. It's brilliant when you first turn it on, too. I do like the cooler, bluish light that it emits, but anyone who has a sleep problem with blue light might not want to put these bulbs in their bedroom.
Gorgeous and perfect for my daughter's room!

Doesn’t fit as expected.  I have one from another brand and it’s great.  I needed a longer one but this doesn’t even hang or close as it should. Definitely not the same quality.  The other one had weights in the bottom and the magnetic closures work great.  This one is not even close to working in the same way

The product arrived as described, but the light did not work. It was a little disappointing because this was a gift. I don't know if it was a battery or light issue, but the rest worked perfectly. I would give 5 stars if everything worked the way it was supposed to, but one of the main features of the product didn't work.

I've had it installed just over a week and I'm returning it because the mist that showers out really isn't nice. It's nowhere near "high pressure" and the diffusion of the water droplets really makes the water cold.
Ten months later, SIX out of NINE have died  (various rooms throughout the house)
These did not work well. Cheap, Chinese junk.

We bought this product to fix our broken screen door. It’s darker than the screen door so it’s very obvious that it’s broken. Anyway it did its job well. We’re not worried anymore that our cat will find a way to escape from our apartment.

I love these bulbs for our playroom, it's a large space with only 2 ceiling bulbs. I've had 75-100 watt equivalent bulbs in there, but they are not bright enough. This one is much better! There is noticeably more light in the room, and still only using 23w (about what the old CFL bulbs used to use at less lumen output!). The color matches our other tungsten colored bulbs as well which is nice. I'm not 100% certain I'm getting 200w equivalent light output, as that should be twice what I'm getting from the 100w equivalents. It's brighter, but maybe closer to the 150w equ claim than the 200. I'm happy with them all the same.
Good small flashlight, but the annoying strobe setting which you constantly run into is...well...annoying.

I'll be honest, I can't vouch for the 2500lm brightness, that seems like a bit of a stretch. It does seem a bit brighter than my 1500lm bulbs that I also got from Amazon, but not incredibly. The color rendition is great, it blends in with my other bulbs nicely.
So far so good, none of them have burnt out yet but I haven't owned them long enough to vouch for that either.
just whatI wanted for this project...and some future ones too

I got these to replace an ugly old fixture that came with our home (50+ years old and very dated looking). It was relatively easy to install, although to be fair I've installed similar lights before.
The only problem however, is how LOPSIDED the light and shadow emitting from it is. There's only a single bulb socket inside, whereas usually these kinds of fixtures have two across from each other. When using modern LED bulbs, the problem gets exaggerated because those bulbs are more directional in their glow. The end result is that it looks like one side is burnt out while the other bright. I think I like it better while off, if I'm being honest. I know this brand also makes a larger base (I think 13"?) that has dual bulbs inside so i'd probably recommend that over this smaller one. I figured the smaller one would make a good option for a hallway, but now I wish I had used a dual.
This item did not work I am highly disappointed

nobody in USA has pipes, hoses or anything that sizes microscopic, so these assorted sizes are JUNK.  better it there were 50 larger washers, say ONE INCH than thousands 1/8 INCH.  come on man.  if you have a garden hose, or an inside plumbing line, or faucet, these washers will not work.  just too small.  suggestion is for seller to put a photo next to a garden hose, or pipe, to show its SIZE mismatch.

Amazing how this works. Easy to push into the wall, except not so easy to push into paneling. It hides behind the picture if you have strung the hanging wire correctly, but it is small enough to hide even on a short wire. Amazing the thing is so light, too.

I like it!.

I have one room that is hard to keep cool. This is the best answer. The cool air lasts quite a while inside a cool house, but not so much outside. It would probably do a lot better in a dry climate rather than this very humid swamp-like air in the deep South. If the heat bothers you as much as me, then this handy little A/C is worth the money.

I am still a bit skeptical so I am cautiously using my counter that it has been put on and still keeping the door closed after two weeks of letting it cure.  The bottom line, the product is OK.

"Trisodium phosphate is a phosphate-based cleaning compound that also acts as a degreaser. It breaks down fibers and removes the "sizing" manufacturers use in new garments. It makes everything look like it's been washed a million times instantly. "
UNFORTUNATELY I found this on the web AFTER I had used it on my very heavy expensive cotton sheets, which do indeed now look like they are a million years old, little wear marks and pin hole and all. VERY sad. Switching Immediately to STPP, which is fine for laundry. Lesson learned.
We bought this for our fish pond to help the Bass catch bugs at night. Works great. Extremely bright and does the job swimmingly!
DOES NOT COME WITH LIGHT FIXTURE...ONLY THE STAND...

I was such in a hurry trying to put my order in that I failed to read description and reviews. I'm organizing daughter's new space for her clothes & I was lacking something for her sox & bows. This is so small!! I could use it for sewing compartment or make up. ALWAYS READ DESCRIPTION AND REVIEWS

We were excited to get this product but when we did we were disappointed that the blue color was not as vibrant as displayed on Amazon. We returned this product so I cannot speak to how they stick or how easy they were to install. We ordered a different product and I can say that they were relatively easy to install and stick well.

I was about to leave this a poor review because I tried it and it never seemed to work the way I expected. Putting it into a fixture, it just turned on and stayed on the whole time. I tried covering the light sensors, I tried turning it on while the light sensors were covered and then uncovering them... no matter what I did, it just seemed to work like a normal light bulb. So I decided to just leave it like a normal light bulb, and then within 24 hours it was turning itself off in the daylight and back on at night. Apparently it doesn't respond right away, but rather takes some time to "calibrate". I suppose they were trying to avoid the frequent on/off when the light changes temporarily thing, but honestly they could have said that in the instructions. But there was no proper instruction booklet or any information that tells you to wait for calibration or anything like that. I wish it were more predictable, looking at the reviews here I see that others had similar experiences. So, if it doesn't work right away, DON'T PANIC! It should eventually sort itself out.

I have kids that frequently forget to close the garage door into our home, which has let in all kinds of humidity in the summer and cold in the winter. It's not a heavy door, I just wanted something to make sure it closes, and this is perfect for that. It's actually a big smaller of a closer mechanism than I expected, but it does the job... as long as my attic fan in the garage is off, that is!
The garage has a fan to blow out hot and humid air, and it sort of creates a vacuum effect when it is running and the door closes, so much so that it refuses to close the last inch without manually pulling it. When the fan is off it works as expected and closes it all the way. If I adjust it more to close harder, it becomes too difficult for my younger kids to open. I'm happy enough with this to leave it for now, it's very good, if not perfect.

I love the way these wall sconces look.  They fit in with our decor (farmhouse) perfectly.  BUT, installing them was very frustrating, as the screws included were not long enough - fortunately we had some that would work,  AND, to replace a bulb, you practically have to take the whole sconce apart!  My grandson spent an hour trying to figure out an easy way to replace a bulb when it burns out, but couldn’t find any - so we’re all dreading that event.  In the meantime, they look great!

Ok, first- this worked fine and added another needed remote opener for our other car. Eventually. I say that because from the term "universal" I was expecting this to be as easy to program as our minivan's built in remote system. Our Honda (and I've seen other cars as well) has a "universal" system that can learn the signal from another remote by putting it into a program mode while pressing the remote button next to it. That's how I originally programmed my Honda's built in button for opening the garage and it has worked great for years! I was hoping this one would be the same type of system, but alas- I had to get a ladder and climb up to garage opener system to find the settings and figure out how to set the remote to work with it.
It did eventually, but I know it could have been easier to setup from experience with other universal systems. That being said, it still did what it claims to do so I can't complain that much.
Good mack, elastic is to short.

The good news is this comes with a good selection of bits, from screwdriver to sockets to drill bits. More than most drill sets would come with, and the case along with those bits seem good quality. However, the quality of the unit seems suspicious. In fact, right out of the box, the battery seemed charged... the light would never change to indicate that it was charging, so I decided to use it for a project. I used it until the battery wore down, then tried to charge it... only to find that the battery charger does not change because it seems to NOT BE CHARGING the battery at all. I'm not sure if the problem is the battery or the charger, but I'm disappointed in the quality control of this product. Out of the box it was broken.
I'm so glad to have this. It is so powerful and easy to handle. Love all the drill head screwdrivers and sockets. I almost feel professional.
Could not get one 3/8 bostich staple out of this thing. Returned immediately and got the H30-8 instead. Hopefully I’ll have better results.
